---
title: "Introduction to Stata"
author: "Yang Liu"
date: Oct. 2025
format: 
   revealjs:
    slide-number: c/t
    width: 1600
    height: 900
    theme: simple
    incremental: true
    scrollable: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
editor: visual
---
## Introduction to Stata {#slide-1}

![Pink flowers and leaves on white background](ppt/media/image7.jpeg "Picture 3")

2025-2026

## Environment {#slide-2}

![A blue background with white squares

Description automatically generated](ppt/media/image8.png "Picture 5")

![A logo with a letter p

Description automatically generated](ppt/media/image9.png "Picture 7")

![A group of blue circles

Description automatically generated](ppt/media/image10.png "Picture 4")

![A blue text on a black background

Description automatically generated](ppt/media/image11.png "Picture 8")

## Objective {#slide-3}

Understand Stata codes

Ask AI to write Stata codes

## Virtual Sites {#slide-4}

Virtual Sites does not save files automatically. 

Virtual Sites sessions will time out after 15 minutes of inactivity.

After a Virtual Sites session is terminated, session data is deleted within 15 minutes.

Getting Started with Virtual Sites / U-M Information and Technology Services (umich.edu)

## Overview {#slide-5}

SPSS

Excel

Stata

SAS

R

Point and click

code

Point and click

Point and click

code

code

Easy

Difficult

Learning

Reproducible

Poor

Great

## Overview {#slide-6}

Input:  RedCap , Qualtrics, Excel

Stata

R

Point and click

code

Point and click

code

Easy

Difficult but easy with AI

Learning

Reproducible

Poor

Great

Output 

## Contents  {#slide-7}

I Stata Basics (15 min)

- 

II Data management (15 min)

- 

III Statistical analysis (15 min)

Course datasets:

employee_0.xls

employee_1.xls

## Slide 8

![](ppt/media/image12.png "Picture 1")

upload datasets

## Slide 9

![](ppt/media/image13.png "Picture 8")

 datasets are uploaded to the folder: Remote Desktop Virtual Drive on  RDWebClient /Uploads

Check if uploading is completed successfully: Stata/SE 18.0 click "File", click "Open..." 

Change  to All Files

## Slide 10

![](ppt/media/image14.png "Picture 2")

## Slide 11

![A screenshot of a computer

AI-generated content may be incorrect.](ppt/media/image15.png "Content Placeholder 4")

## I Stata Basics-Window {#slide-12}

Command

Results

History

Variables

Properties

![](ppt/media/image16.png "Picture 4")

## Basics -- get ready  {#slide-13}

Set working directory (click)

File → change working directory : 

cd \"C:\\Users\\youruniquename\\Documents\"

Create Log file:  stata  (click)

Documentation 

![](ppt/media/image17.png "Picture 4")

![](ppt/media/image18.png "Picture 5")

## Basics - Open data {#slide-14}

Import data (click)

File → import → Excel spreadsheet

employee_0.xls

Check "import first row as variable names"

File → save

If use virtual site, type  save employee_0

![](ppt/media/image19.png "Picture 4")

![](ppt/media/image20.png "Picture 3")

Open existing data in working directory(command)

Type  use employee_0

## Stata Command {#slide-15}

. import excel \"\\\\tsclient\\Remote Desktop Virtual Drive\\Uploads\\employee_0.xls\", sheet(\"Sheet1\")  firstrow

## Ask AI to help {#slide-16}

Q: Hi! how can  i  import excel data into Stata?

![A blue text on a black background

Description automatically generated](ppt/media/image11.png "Picture 4")

## Basics - inspect data {#slide-17}

View data (click)

Data → Data Editor → Data Editor (Browse)

Browse data table, variables

![](ppt/media/image17.png "Picture 3")

## Basics - Describe variables {#slide-18}

Command:  describe

#observations

#variables

Variable name

Variable label

Output: 

![](ppt/media/image21.png "Picture 6")

![](ppt/media/image20.png "Picture 3")

## Invite AI to the table {#slide-19}

Q: Hi! I'm working on a dataset " employee_o " using Stata: 

Can you summarize my data?

![](ppt/media/image21.png "Picture 3")

![A blue text on a black background

Description automatically generated](ppt/media/image11.png "Picture 4")

## Summary Remarks {#slide-20}

UM-GPT: The dataset captures demographic information (gender,  bdate , minority), employment-related data ( jobcat ,  jobtime ), and financial information (salary,  salbegin ).

......

......

UM-GPT: Would you like any specific analysis or further details on any of these variables?

![A blue text on a black background

Description automatically generated](ppt/media/image11.png "Picture 4")

## Basics - Describe data contents {#slide-21}

Command:  codebook

Categorical 

value

Missing data

Frequency

Output: 

![](ppt/media/image22.png "Picture 4")

## Basics - Describe data contents {#slide-22}

Command:  codebook

continuous

value

range

Missing data

Mean,  sd , percentile

Output: 

![](ppt/media/image23.png "Picture 5")

## Exercise 5 min {#slide-23}

Type answers in chat:

\# of variables  

\# of observations 

Mean salary

How many are minority

Hint:  describe    codebook   

![Hourglass on white background](ppt/media/image24.jpeg "Picture 4")

## I Recap {#slide-24}

Set working directory (click)

Create log (click)

Import . xls  .csv .txt data (click)  or open . dta  data (click)

View data (click)

Describe data (command)

describe

codebook

## II Data management {#slide-25}

Merge two datasets  

Edit variables

Subset data

## Slide 26

\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\--

Variable  name                

\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\--

id                                      

gender                              

bdate                                  

educ                                  

jobcat                                

salary                               

salbegin                            

jobtime                             

minority                           

Contains data from employee_0.dta

Contains data from employee_1.dta

\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\--

Variable  name                

\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\--

id 

prevexp

                                     

## Merge {#slide-27}

Import employee_1.xls (click) check "import first row as variable names"

save employee_1

use employee_0

merge 1:1 id using employee_1

describe

save employee

## Slide 28

![](ppt/media/image25.png "Picture 4")

## Label variables {#slide-29}

Variable name         Variable label

\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\--

id                              Employment Code

gender                      Gender

bdate                        Date of Birth

educ                        Educational Level (years)

jobcat                        Employment Category

salary                      Current Salary

salbegin                   Beginning Salary

jobtime                    Months since Hire

prevexp                    Previous Experience (months)

minority                    Minority  Classification

## Label variables {#slide-30}

Select variable (click)

unlock property (click)

Label variables (click)

Lock property (click)

Command:

label var  name  " label "

Example:

label var  id  " Employment Code "

![](ppt/media/image26.png "Picture 9")

## Label values {#slide-31}

![](ppt/media/image27.png "Picture 4")

codebook  jobcat

## Label values {#slide-32}

Select variable (click)

unlock property (click)

Value label (click ... )

Create label and choose 

Lock property (click)

Command:

Example:

label define  cat  1 \"clerical\" 2 \"custodial\" 3 \"manager"

label values  jobcat   cat

![](ppt/media/image28.png "Picture 4")

## Label values {#slide-33}

![](ppt/media/image29.png "Picture 4")

Command:  codebook  jobcat

Output: 

## Add variables {#slide-34}

Edit data (click)

Data → Create or change data→ create new variable

Variable type: int

Variable name : age

Specify a value or an expression:

 ( mdy (10,22,1995) -  bdate )/365.25

save

Command:

  gen int  age  = ( mdy (10,22,1995) -  bdate )/365.25

save employee, replace

![](ppt/media/image30.png "Picture 7")

## Ask AI to write codes {#slide-35}

Q: Hi! The variable " bdate " is date of birth, my data was collected on 10-22-1995, can you create a new variable "age"? Please write Stata codes. 

![A blue text on a black background

Description automatically generated](ppt/media/image11.png "Picture 4")

## Ask AI to troubleshoot {#slide-36}

Q: Hi! I got this error message in Stata:

...

...

Can you fix it? 

![A blue text on a black background

Description automatically generated](ppt/media/image11.png "Picture 4")

## Keep or drop variables {#slide-37}

Window-variables → select variable → right click → keep or drop

Command:

keep 

Drop

Example:  drop  bdate

Example:   keep  id age salary  jobcat   prevexp

Be careful 

clear  (tell Stata to clear memory and not to save changes)

save employee, replace  ( tell Stata to save changes and overwrite employee)

save  employee_new   ( tell Stata to save as a new file)

## Keep or drop observations {#slide-38}

Data → create or change data → add or insert observations (click)

Data → create or change data → drop or keep observations (click)

Command:

keep drop

Example:   keep if  gender  == "f"

Example:   keep if  salary  \> 50000

Be careful 

clear  (tell Stata to clear memory and not to save changes)

save employee, replace  ( tell Stata to save changes and overwrite employee)

save  employee_new   ( tell Stata to save as a new file)

## Exercise 5 min {#slide-39}

1. Label variable educ:  Education level (years)

2. Type answers in chat:  mean age of female employee

3. Type answers in chat: median salary of female employee

![Hourglass on white background](ppt/media/image24.jpeg "Picture 4")

## II Recap {#slide-40}

Manage data:  merge ,  keep ,  drop

Manage variables:  label , generate ( gen ), convert 

Why are we doing this?

## III Statistical analysis {#slide-41}

Univariate (summary statistics) -- single variable

Continuous-  sum

Visualize -  hist

Categorical-  tab

Visualize-   bar graph

Bivariate -- two variables

Chi-Square test

Wilcoxon rank sum test

## Slide 42

![](ppt/media/image31.png "Picture 3")

Data type

age

educ 

Jobtime

Prevexp

salary

salbegin

gender

jobcat

minority

Continuous

Categorical

## Continuous - Summarize  {#slide-43}

Command:  summarize  or  sum

Numeric 

Mean,  sd , min, max

![](ppt/media/image32.png "Picture 6")

Example 2 :  sum  salary , detail 

![](ppt/media/image33.png "Picture 4")

Example 1 :  sum  salary  

Median(IQR): 50%(25%, 75%)

extra : table of summary statistics

tabstat   salary , s(median, p25, p75)

tabstat   salary  salbegin , s(median, p25, p75) 

## Visualize continuous data {#slide-44}

Histogram 

Distribution of data

Command:  hist

Example:  hist  salary

![](ppt/media/image34.emf "Picture 4")

Output: 

## Categorical-tab {#slide-45}

![](ppt/media/image35.png "Picture 5")

Command:  tab

categorical

frequency

percentage

Output: 

Example :  tab  jobcat

Extra:

Cross tabulation :  tab  jobcat  gender

                       tab   jobcat  gender, column row

Tabulate 2 or more variables:  tab1   jobcat  gender

## Visualize categorical data {#slide-46}

Bar graph 

Command:  graph bar

Example:  graph bar, over( jobcat )

Output: 

![](ppt/media/image36.emf "Picture 8")

## Slide 47

Example of basic summary statistics: 

##  Chi-Square test {#slide-48}

Bivariate analysis

Categorical

Association between two  categorical  variables

Example: Run a crosstab between gender and  jobcat

Command:  tab  gender  jobcat

Do a chi-square test to examine whether job status is different between males and females.

Command:  tab  gender  jobcat , row chi

 tab  gender  jobcat ,  chi2

![](ppt/media/image40.png "Picture 6")

Output: 

## Ask AI to write codes {#slide-49}

Q: I want to run  a chi-square test to examine whether job status is different between males and females. Can you write Stata codes? 

![A blue text on a black background

Description automatically generated](ppt/media/image11.png "Picture 4")

## Ask AI to interpret results {#slide-50}

Q: This is the chi-square test output:

...

...

      Can you interpret it? 

![A blue text on a black background

Description automatically generated](ppt/media/image11.png "Picture 4")

## Interpretation {#slide-51}

UM-GPT

The distribution of job categories is different between genders. For example, there are relatively more males in job category 3 compared to females, and no females are in job category 2 while some males are.

The Pearson chi-square statistic is 79.2771, which is a measure of how much the observed counts deviate from the expected counts if gender and job category were independent.

The p-value is less than 0.001 (commonly denoted as 0.000 in outputs), which is well below any typical significance level (e.g., 0.05)

![A blue text on a black background

Description automatically generated](ppt/media/image11.png "Picture 3")

## Conclusion {#slide-52}

UM-GPT:

Statistical Significance: With a p-value \< 0.05, we reject the null hypothesis that job category and gender are independent. This result suggests that there is a statistically significant association between gender and job category. Specifically, the distribution of job categories is different for males and females in your sample.

![A blue text on a black background

Description automatically generated](ppt/media/image11.png "Picture 3")

## Continue the conversation {#slide-53}

Q: Thanks! one of the cell count is less than 5 (number of female in category 2). do you think Fisher\'s exact test is more appropriate than chi-square test in this case?

![A blue text on a black background

Description automatically generated](ppt/media/image11.png "Picture 3")

## Wilcoxon rank sum test {#slide-54}

Bivariate analysis

One continuous variable and one Categorical variable

Non-parametric test

Compare distribution of a continuous variable between two population

Example: distribution of salary for the two categories defined by gender

Command:  ranksum   salary , by( gender )

![](ppt/media/image41.png "Picture 4")

Output: 

## Slide 55

![](ppt/media/image42.png "Picture 4")

Example of bivariate analysis: 

## Exercise 5 min {#slide-56}

1 . Obtain mean,  sd , min, max, median, p25, p75 of salary. 

2. Obtain frequency and percent of gender. Type n(%) of female in chat.

3. Run a crosstab between  jobcat  and minority. Do a chi-square test to examine whether job status is different between minority employees and other. 

4. Keep observations that salary is greater than \$30000. Run a Wilcoxon rank sum test to examine whether the distribution of age is different between female and male. 

![Hourglass on white background](ppt/media/image24.jpeg "Picture 4")

## III Recap {#slide-57}

Univariate analysis

Continuous  sum

Categorical  tab

Bivariate analysis

2 categorical variables - Chi-Square test

1 categorical and 1 continuous variable - Wilcoxon rank sum test

## Recap {#slide-58}

- Stata Basics 

<!-- -->

- Import, open, inspect data

<!-- -->

-  Data management 

<!-- -->

- Merge datasets, tidy data

<!-- -->

-  Statistical analysis 

<!-- -->

- Univariate: describe and visualize
- Bivariate: Chi-square test, Wilcoxon test

<!-- -->

- 

## Syntax {#slide-59}

describe   describe  variables

codebook    describe data contents

tab    frequency table -categorical variable

sum    summary statistics -continuous variable

## Do file {#slide-60}

![](ppt/media/image43.png "Picture 4")

![](ppt/media/image43.png "Picture 6")

## Resources {#slide-61}

Google

https://www.princeton.edu/\~otorres/StataTutorial.pdf

https://stats.oarc.ucla.edu/stata/modules/

https://www.youtube.com/user/statacorp

https://blog.uvm.edu/tbplante/2019/07/11/make-a-table-1-in-stata-in-no-time-with-table1_mc/

https://www.linkedin.com/learning/introduction-to-stata-15/why-you-should-use-stata?u=42573940

Dropbox: OBGYN-Stats

https://www.dropbox.com/sh/ddoeg3oxmoekjas/AACT7yA5vuRhezvHJndIQSHqa?dl=0

Order Stata at UM:

https://software.its.umich.edu/Portal/Item/View/2499

AI User Guide:

https://midas.umich.edu/research/research-resources/generative-ai-hub/users-guide/

## Stata Support {#slide-62}

Email:  ylyl@med.umich.edu  

## Continuous variable {#slide-63}

Check normality

swilk  var1 var2 var3 

Data:  swilk  p\<0.05, non-normal distribution, Median(IQR)

tabstat  var1 var2 var3, s(median, p25, p75)

Data:  swilk  \>=0.05, normal distribution, Mean,  sd

tabstat  var1 var2 var3, s(mean,  sd )

By group

tabstat  var1 var2 var3, s(median, p25, p75) by(arm)

tabstat  var1 var2 var3, s(mean,  sd ) by(arm)

## Categorical variable {#slide-64}

Stata codes

tab var

tab1 var1 var2

tab var1 var2, column row

tab var1 var2, sum(var3)

  

## Table {#slide-65}

Stata codes

ssc  install table1_mc

table1_mc, vars( age   contn  %4.0f \\  sex0m1f  bin %4.0f \\  race0w1b  bin %4.0f \\  region123  cate %4.0f \\  educ1234  cat %4.0f \\  income1234  cat %4.0f \\  sbp   conts  %4.0f \\   dbp   conts  %4.0f )   onecol  missing total(before) saving(\"table 1.xlsx\", replace)

table1_mc, by( mygroup ) vars( age   contn  %4.0f \\  sex0m1f  bin %4.0f \\  race0w1b  bin %4.0f \\  region123  cate %4.0f \\  educ1234  cat %4.0f \\  income1234  cat %4.0f \\  sbp   conts  %4.0f \\   dbp   conts  %4.0f )   onecol  missing total(before) saving(\"table 2.xlsx\", replace)
